diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Gastric Outlet Obstruction due to Malignant Intrinsic Short Stenosis in D1 secondary to Metastatic Cholangiocarcinoma
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with epigastric pain and vomiting, with imaging revealing a gastric outlet obstruction. An endoscopy found malignant intrinsic short stenosis in the first part of the duodenum, confirming the obstruction's cause. Additionally, the patient was also found to have a pulmonary embolism during the course of evaluation.
